channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸 Pentagon Seeks Additional Funding as Iran War Costs Exceed $29 Billion
item.description: The US Department of Defense has requested supplemental funding as costs for the Iran conflict have risen from $25 billion to over $29 billion in just two weeks, driven by equipment repairs, replacements, and ongoing operational expenses.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO to Create Vetted Counter-Drone Vendor Pool for Member Nations
item.description: NATO is establishing a common funding mechanism and approved vendor pool for counter-UAS systems, allowing member nations to test systems before procurement decisions.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Announces Propellant Breakthrough for Yildirimhan Long-Range Ballistic Missile
item.description: Turkish defense officials have announced a significant advancement in solid propellant technology for the Yildirimhan long-range ballistic missile program, boosting domestic missile development capabilities.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Trump Says Russia-Ukraine War End Is Very Close
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that he believes the Russia-Ukraine war is nearing an end and that Moscow and Kyiv are moving closer to a settlement, amid ongoing diplomatic efforts.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Gas Facility 1,500km From Border
item.description: Ukrainian forces carried out drone strikes on a gas facility in Russia's Orenburg Oblast, more than 1,500 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, demonstrating continued deep-strike capabilities after the Victory Day ceasefire ended.

item.title: 🚢☢️🇰🇵 Russian ship that sank near Spain may have been carrying nuclear reactors to North Korea
item.description: A Russian vessel that sank in the Mediterranean may have been transporting nuclear reactor components destined for North Korea, raising concerns about illicit nuclear proliferation and sanctions evasion.

item.title: 🦠 French hantavirus patient on artificial lung as outbreak spreads to 11 cases
item.description: A French woman infected with hantavirus on the MV Hondius cruise ship is in critical condition on an artificial lung, as the total number of confirmed cases grows to 11, prompting WHO to urge countries to prepare for more.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump-Xi Iran Sanctions Could Reshape Trade Landscape
item.description: New sanctions discussed between Trump and Xi Jinping targeting Iran could significantly impact global trade routes and energy markets as both leaders navigate the complex geopolitical landscape.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Widow Detained by ICE in Immigration Case
item.description: A French widow has been detained by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, highlighting increased immigration enforcement under the current administration and raising diplomatic concerns.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Secretly Attacked Iran During War, Raising Gulf State Concerns
item.description: The United Arab Emirates conducted covert strikes on Iranian targets during the recent conflict, a move that risks drawing Gulf states into direct confrontation with Iran and destabilizing regional security arrangements.

item.title: 🌍 Internal Displacements Hit Record High in 2025 Amid Violence and Conflict
item.description: Global internal displacements reached unprecedented levels in 2025, driven by armed conflicts, violence, and climate-related disasters, with humanitarian organizations warning of a worsening crisis.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Sent Iron Dome Batteries and Personnel to UAE, US Envoy Confirms
item.description: Israel deployed Iron Dome anti-missile systems and military personnel to the United Arab Emirates during the Iran conflict, marking a significant escalation in Israel-UAE defense cooperation confirmed by the US ambassador.

item.title: 🇷🇸 Serbia Hosts First Joint Military Exercise With NATO
item.description: Serbia conducted its first joint military exercise with NATO forces, marking a potential shift in the Balkan nation's military alignment despite its traditional ties to Russia.

item.title: 💰 Kevin Warsh Confirmed to US Federal Reserve Board in Close Senate Vote
item.description: Kevin Warsh was confirmed to the Federal Reserve Board of Governors after a closely contested Senate vote, giving him significant influence over US monetary policy as the economy navigates post-war inflation pressures.

item.title: ⚔️ Allies Surpassed US in Military Budget Purchasing Power Last Year
item.description: A new report shows that US allies collectively surpassed American military purchasing power last year, reflecting increased defense spending in Europe and Asia amid global security concerns.

item.title: 🇮🇷 IRGC Navy Expands Definition of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Navy has significantly expanded its claimed definition of the Strait of Hormuz, potentially creating new flashpoints for naval confrontation in one of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoints.

item.title: 🚀 Russia Plans Operational Deployment of Sarmat ICBM This Year
item.description: Russia announced plans to deploy the Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile operationally this year, advancing its strategic nuclear modernization despite ongoing economic pressures from sanctions and the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's Yuan Hits Three-Year High Before Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China's yuan reached a three-year high against the dollar just days before the scheduled Trump-Xi summit, as markets anticipate potential agreements on trade and economic cooperation between the two leaders.

item.title: 💰 Trump's 'Golden Dome' Missile Shield Estimated to Cost $12 Trillion
item.description: A new cost analysis estimates President Trump's proposed 'Golden Dome' missile defense system could cost up to $12 trillion over its lifetime, raising questions about feasibility and budget priorities.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Sanctions Hamas Leaders and Israeli Settlers
item.description: The European Union imposed sanctions on both Hamas leaders and extremist Israeli settlers, marking a significant shift in EU policy toward the Israel-Palestine conflict with penalties on both sides.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Keir Starmer Becomes UK Prime Minister
item.description: Keir Starmer has become the new Prime Minister of the United Kingdom following electoral victory, promising to navigate post-Brexit challenges and redefine Britain's role in European security.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Hezbollah Fires Explosive Drones After Medics Killed in Lebanon
item.description: Hezbollah launched explosive drones at Israel after two Lebanon medical personnel were reported killed, escalating cross-border tensions amid the broader regional conflict involving Iran.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Covertly Struck Iran During War, Sources Say
item.description: Saudi Arabia conducted multiple unpublicized strikes on Iranian targets during the Middle East war in retaliation for attacks on the kingdom, with both sides reaching a diplomatic understanding to de-escalate, according to Iranian and Western officials.

item.title: 📰 Israel Advances to Eurovision Finals Amid Boycott Calls
item.description: Israel advanced to the Eurovision Song Contest finals despite intense international boycott calls and protests over its military actions, with performances met with both cheers and boos from the audience.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Strikes Kryvyi Rih After Ceasefire Ends, Killing Two
item.description: Russian forces struck Kryvyi Rih in central Ukraine just one day after the Victory Day ceasefire ended, killing two people and injuring four others including a 9-month-old infant in critical condition.
